headline: Soccer Doc 'The Match' Scores Big at Cannes with International Sales Coup
lede: A riveting documentary exploring the infamous 1986 World Cup quarter-final clash between Argentina and England is set to become a global talking point after securing key international distribution.
body:
Round 12 has clinched international sales rights for Juan Cabral and Santiago Franco's electrifying documentary 'The Match', positioning the film as a must-watch title at this year's Cannes Film Festival.

The documentary chronicles the legendary quarter-final match between Argentina and England — immortalized by Diego Maradona's controversial 'Hand of God' goal — with narration from football icons Gary Lineker and Jorge Valdano. Their firsthand perspectives promise to deliver unprecedented insight into one of soccer's most mythologized moments.

Round 12's strategic acquisition marks a significant milestone for the sports-focused label, which was recently created through a partnership between Mediawan and Goodfellas. The film represents their first official Cannes selection, signaling an ambitious expansion into premium sports documentary territory.

Cabral and Franco's approach reportedly blends archival footage, player interviews, and nuanced historical context to deconstruct a match that transcended mere athletic competition. The film promises to explore the geopolitical tensions between Argentina and England in the mid-1980s, positioning the soccer match as a metaphorical battlefield.
key_facts: Narrated by Gary Lineker and Jorge Valdano
Directed by Juan Cabral and Santiago Franco
